preamble: The historical transformation of a voiceless velar stop [k] into a voiceless retroflex affricate [ʈʂ]. This complex shift is a defining feature in the development of the Polish and Mandarin consonant systems, typically mediated by an intermediate palatalization stage.

examples.note: The First Palatalization of Velars turned *k into *č, which later retracted to the retroflex [ʈʂ] (written 'cz') in Polish.
In certain historical stages and northern dialects, palatalized velars shifted toward the retroflex position to maintain contrast.

sources: Swan, O. E. (2002). A Grammar of Contemporary Polish.
